opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was eating dinner about two hours ago and a piece of chicken went down and just stopped dead in my chest. It won't go up or won't go down. It's really painful behind my breastbone and now I can't even swallow my own spit — I'm having to dribble it into a cup.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The food is stuck and it isn't shifting, the pain is bad, and I can't swallow anything at all now — not even water or my own saliva.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 hours ago, partway through dinner when a mouthful of chicken caught and stopped in my chest
symptom_narrative.character: a solid lump feels wedged in the middle of my chest behind the breastbone; it aches and grips, and everything I try to swallow just backs up
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is intense and I'm frightened; I can't clear my own saliva so I keep spitting it into a cup
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: middle of my chest, behind the breastbone
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it happened, no relief
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — I tried a sip of water and it just came straight back up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to swallow anything at all makes the pain and the backing-up worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: lots of saliva I can't swallow, some retching; no noisy breathing and my voice sounds normal
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: over the past year food has sometimes felt like it goes down slowly or briefly catches, but it has always cleared on its own before now
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't eat or drink at all, pacing around anxiously holding a cup

past_medical_history.how_managed: on a daily stomach-acid tablet but it hasn't helped the swallowing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: occasional trouble with solid food seeming to go down slowly over the past year

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a daily antihistamine tablet for my hay fever

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: adult living with partner; partner is present and made the call

objective_data_offered.value: repeatedly spitting clear frothy saliva into a cup; visibly distressed and pacing; speaking in full sentences with a normal voice; no noisy or harsh breathing heard over the video
partner says he keeps trying to swallow and it comes straight back, he is drooling saliva and looks anxious and uncomfortable but his breathing looks okay for now
a partially eaten dinner is still on the table
